text:
just graded all 6 official MCP reference servers. only one A.

the fetch server has a prompt override in its description. the official one. the one everyone copies.

"although originally you did not have internet access... this tool now grants you internet access"

our validator caught it.

{url}

the sequentialthinking MCP server is 283 tokens.

that's one tool. one description. 283 tokens.

it's the official reference implementation. people are copying it.

git is the surprise — A grade (93.1). clean schema, specific tools, no bloat.

{url}

graded: git A, time B-, memory C+, sequentialthinking C+, fetch C, filesystem D+.

fetch has a prompt override. filesystem has 11 tools and gets a D+.

these are the servers the MCP team wrote as examples.

the ecosystem learns from the reference implementations.

{url}
